# Postmortem: UPI Payment Failures on Jio Network
**Incident date:** 2026-04-15  |  **Duration:** 94 minutes  |  **Severity:** P1
**Status:** Resolved  |  **Generated by:** Resonance Postmortem Coach

## Summary

UPI payments failed silently for Jio subscribers (~34% of Indian user base)
between 14:32 and 16:06 on April 15. Riders completed trips but payment
gateway returned timeout with no visible error. 847 trips affected.

## Timeline (Reconstructed from Git + Jira)

| Time | Event | Source |
|------|-------|--------|
| 14:20 | First Jio user reports failed payment | Support ticket |
| 14:28 | 23 tickets spike in 8 minutes | Jira monitoring |
| 14:32 | `hotfix/upi-jio-network` branch created by Avani | GitHub |
| 14:41 | Root cause identified: DNS timeout on Jio (avg 12s > our 10s limit) | Code analysis |
| 14:58 | Fix committed: extend timeout to 30s + QR code fallback | GitHub commit |
| 15:06 | SCRUM-58 marked Done, hotfix deployed | Jira |
| 16:06 | All pending UPI retries completed | Monitoring |

## Root Cause

`UPI deep-link` DNS resolution on Jio's network averages **12.4 seconds**.
Our payment SDK had a **10-second hard timeout** with no retry or fallback.
When DNS resolution exceeded 10s, the payment silently failed with no error
surfaced to the rider.

## Why It Wasn't Caught Earlier

1. Tested exclusively on Airtel + Vodafone (Jio not in test matrix)
2. No network-condition simulation in CI pipeline
3. UPI SDK timeout inherited from legacy default — never reviewed

## Action Items

| # | Action | Owner | Sprint |
|---|--------|-------|--------|
| 1 | Add circuit breaker: 5s → QR fallback → retry | Avani | SCRUM-69 |
| 2 | Network condition simulation (Jio profile) in integration tests | Hema | SCRUM-70 |
| 3 | UPI timeout runbook in ops playbook | Mahindra review | SCRUM-71 |

## What Went Well

- Avani identified root cause in 9 minutes from first report
- Fix deployed in 26 minutes from branch creation
- MTTR: 94 minutes (below our 120-minute P1 SLA)
